files.contents:
# Hello Colors App

This project is an Express + Tailwind app that serves a small frontend where you can change the displayed "Hello World" text color through a UI. The color is stored in memory on the server (so it resets when the server restarts).

Run steps:

1. Install dependencies:

   npm install

2. Build Tailwind CSS once (produces public/dist/styles.css):

   npm run build:css

   Note: For development you can run the `dev` script which will concurrently run nodemon and tailwind in watch mode.

3. Start the server (serves files from public/):

   npm start

4. For development (auto-restart server + watch Tailwind):

   npm run dev

Testing:

- Run the automated test that checks the API and static file serving:

  npm test

Notes:
- The server keeps the current color in memory and provides two endpoints:
  - GET /api/color -> { color: "#rrggbb" }
  - POST /api/color (JSON body: { color: "#rrggbb" }) -> updates the current color
- The frontend (public/index.html) uses a color input and an example text to demonstrate the change.

Hello World

The color is stored on the server in memory (resets when server restarts).

/* Minimal compiled CSS placeholder. In development run `npm run build:css` to produce a real Tailwind build. */
body { margin:0; }

notes: Start the server with `npm start` and open http://localhost:3000 to use the UI.
Run `npm test` to execute the included automated tests which verify the API and static file serving.
Tailwind is configured; run `npm run build:css` to produce a full Tailwind CSS file at public/dist/styles.css. A minimal placeholder CSS is included so the app works without building Tailwind.
